info.title: OpenHelpDesk Mobile API
info.description:
REST API for the OpenHelpDesk helpdesk system. Designed for Android and iOS clients.

## Authentication
All endpoints (except `POST /auth/login`) require a Bearer token in the
`Authorization` header:
```
Authorization: Bearer <token>
```
Obtain a token by calling `POST /auth/login`. Revoke it with `POST /auth/logout`.

## Roles
| Role    | Access                                          |
|---------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `admin` | Full access to all endpoints                    |
| `agent` | All agent endpoints; sees tickets per group membership |
| `user`  | Own tickets only; no internal notes or agent data |

## Pagination
List endpoints return a consistent envelope:
```json
{ "data": [...], "total": 100, "page": 1, "per_page": 25, "last_page": 4 }
```

tags.name: Tickets
tags.description: Create, view, update and reply to tickets

tags.name: Knowledge Base
tags.description: Browse KB categories, folders and articles

tags.name: Push
tags.description: Register device tokens for native push notifications

UserMin.description: Compact user object used in nested contexts

user_id.description: null = global; non-null = personal to that agent

body.example: Please visit https://... to reset your password.

TicketSummary.description: Compact ticket row returned in list and dashboard endpoints

TicketDetail.description: Full ticket object returned by GET /tickets/{id}

is_watching.description: Whether the current user is watching this ticket

details.example: Thanks, I'll look into this right away.

is_internal.description: True = internal note (not visible to users)

user.description: Author of the entry (null for system events)

comment_excerpt.description: First 200 characters of the comment that triggered the mention

post.summary: Log in and receive a Bearer token

device_name.description: Human-readable label stored with the token

totp_code.description: Six-digit TOTP code. Required only when the account has two-factor authentication enabled. The key `code` is accepted as an alias.

401.description: Invalid credentials, or a two-factor code is required/incorrect. When 2FA is required the body carries `totp_required: true` — resubmit with `totp_code`.

totp_required.description: Present and true when the account has 2FA enabled and a valid code was not supplied

value.error: A two-factor authentication code is required.

429.description: Login throttled — 5 failures per email or 10 per IP within a rolling 15-minute window

example.error: Too many failed login attempts. Please try again in a few minutes.

post.description: Issues a new token and atomically invalidates the one presented in the `Authorization` header. Call before the 90-day expiry to stay signed in without re-entering credentials. If the new token cannot be stored the old one is preserved.

expires_at.description: When the new token expires (90 days out)

get.description: Returns ticket counts and 10 most recent open tickets. Respects group-based visibility for agents.

unassigned.description: Open/in-progress/pending tickets with no agent assigned

my_tickets.description: Open/in-progress/pending tickets assigned to the current user

resolved_today.description: Tickets resolved on the current calendar day

get.description:
Paginated ticket list with filtering and sorting.
- **Admins** see all tickets.
- **Agents** see tickets belonging to their groups (or all, if not in any group).
- **Users** see only tickets they created.

parameters.name: assigned_to
parameters.description: Agent user ID, or the string "me" for the current user

parameters.name: unassigned
parameters.description: Set to 1 to show only unassigned tickets

post.description:
Creates a ticket on behalf of the authenticated user.
- `group_id`, `assigned_to`, and `due_date` are only accepted from agents/admins.
- If `priority_id` is provided, SLA due dates are initialised automatically.

description.example: The HP LaserJet near the reference desk won't power on.

get.description: Returns the ticket with tags, CC users, watchers, and `is_watching` flag.

post.description:
All fields are optional — only those present in the body are changed.
Each change is logged to the ticket timeline automatically.
Status changes also trigger SLA pause/resume and CSAT survey sending where applicable.

group_id.description: Set to null to remove group

get.description:
Returns all events on the ticket in chronological order.
- **Users** always receive public entries only (`is_internal = false`).
- Agents/admins can request internal notes with `include_internal=1`.

parameters.name: include_internal
parameters.description: Set to 1 to include internal notes (agent/admin only)

post.summary: Post a reply or internal note
post.description:
Adds a comment to the ticket timeline.

- `is_internal` and `status_after` are accepted from agents/admins only.
- Public replies trigger email notifications to the creator, CC users, and watchers.
- `@mention` processing runs automatically on the message body.
- If this is the first public reply by a non-creator, the first-response SLA time is recorded.

message.example: I've ordered a replacement toner — should arrive tomorrow.

is_internal.description: Agent/admin only. True = visible to agents only.

status_after.description: Agent/admin only. Change the ticket status in the same step.

post.description: The only multipart/form-data endpoint. Send one file as `attachments`, or several as repeated `attachments[]` fields. Files are validated against the server's allow-list (`UPLOAD_ALLOWED_TYPES`) by their actual detected MIME type and against the size cap (`UPLOAD_MAX_SIZE`, default 20 MB). Optionally pass `timeline_id` to hang the files off an existing reply/note on this ticket.

attachments.description: One or more files (field name `attachments`, or `attachments[]` for several)

timeline_id.description: Optional — attach to an existing reply/note on this ticket

get.description: Streams the raw file bytes (not JSON) with a `Content-Disposition: attachment` header. Anyone who can access the parent ticket can download its attachments.

post.description:
Register (or refresh) the APNs/FCM token for the current user's device. Tokens are unique server-side — re-registering the same token updates the row in place and reassigns it to the current user if needed.

**Note:** push *delivery* is not implemented yet (see the API guide's "Known gaps"); this stores the token and is a no-op on the send side until provider credentials are configured.
Removes the device token (e.g. on sign-out). Scoped to the caller — you can only remove your own device's token.

get.description: Users only see categories where `is_public = true`.
Users only see articles in public categories.

get.summary: Get a single KB article (full body)

parameters.name: read
parameters.description: Filter by read state (omit for all)

unread.description: Total unread count (regardless of current filter)

get.description: Returns global snippets (`user_id = null`) plus the current user's personal ones.

Returns all lookup tables in a single request, suitable for caching on app startup.
The `agents` array is only populated for agents and admins.

get.summary: Search users by name or email (agent/admin only)
get.description: Returns up to 10 matching users. Useful for CC / assignment pickers.
